questionText: At the sticker adventure, badge making starts at 2:35 p.m. and lasts 18 minutes. Then there is a 12-minute snack break. Treasure stamp hunting must finish exactly at 3:30 p.m. Someone reasons like this: 18 minutes and 12 minutes together make 30 minutes, so 2:35 p.m. plus 30 minutes is 3:05 p.m. Then there are 25 minutes left until 3:30 p.m., so treasure stamp hunting lasts 37 minutes. What is the correct length of treasure stamp hunting?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can solve real-life problems involving timetables and schedules
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: Duration & Schedules, aligned to Students can solve real-life problems involving timetables and schedules.
explanation: First find when treasure stamp hunting starts. Badge making ends at 2:53 p.m. because 2:35 p.m. + 18 minutes = 2:53 p.m. After the 12-minute snack break, it starts at 3:05 p.m. From 3:05 p.m. to 3:30 p.m. is 25 minutes, so the correct answer is 25 minutes. 37 minutes is incorrect because it adds the 12-minute break again after already using it to find the 3:05 p.m. start time. 42 minutes comes from subtracting only the 18-minute badge-making time from 3:30 p.m. and forgetting the 12-minute break. 55 minutes comes from treating all the time from 2:35 p.m. to 3:30 p.m. as treasure stamp hunting and ignoring the earlier activities.
